boardQuestion: Which AI governance claims would fail a buyer or investor trust review first?
publicAssertion: Agent governance, rollout, and exception control evidence is review-ready.
evidenceReality: Rollback, exception-routing, and evaluation review proof still needs a freshness refresh.
driftHeadline: AI governance reads stronger in public than it does in the live proof room.
driftSignal: Exception-routing and rollback evidence is older than the trust language implies.
missingProof: Fresh operating exports for exception handling, evaluation review, and rollback outcomes
headline: AI governance needs fresher proof before it should anchor trust review.
nextMove: Refresh the operating evidence and narrow the highest-confidence AI claim until the proof room catches up.

boardQuestion: Which identity trust claims are closest to clean closure?
publicAssertion: Identity reviews, guest access, and mobile protection controls are audit-ready.
evidenceReality: One guest-access proof export is aging, but the assertion is otherwise aligned to live evidence.
driftHeadline: Identity is the healthiest assertion family, with only minor freshness cleanup left.
driftSignal: Guest-review evidence is slightly older than ideal, but ownership is clear.
headline: Identity can close quickly with a small evidence refresh.
nextMove: Refresh the guest-access export and mark this assertion family ready for external reuse.

boardQuestion: Where will platform and recovery trust review ask for harder evidence?
publicAssertion: Hosting, release safety, backup, and runtime hardening are connected enough for diligence.
evidenceReality: Backup readiness, release rollback, and runtime-hardening proof are still fragmented.
driftHeadline: Platform trust needs escalation because the evidence chain is still fragmented.
driftSignal: Backup readiness, release rollback, and hosted-validator evidence are still loosely linked.
missingProof: One end-to-end packet linking hosting controls to release and recovery proof
headline: Platform trust needs escalation because the evidence chain is still fragmented.
nextMove: Escalate one owner to assemble the hosting-to-recovery proof chain.

boardQuestion: Which trust assertion should anchor the rest of the external proof layer?
publicAssertion: Security questionnaire, trust-center, and proof reuse are buyer-ready.
evidenceReality: Questionnaire reuse is strong, but still benefits from a clearer proof index.
driftHeadline: This trust assertion is structurally strong and mainly needs ongoing hygiene.
driftSignal: Questionnaire reuse still benefits from a more explicit proof index.
missingProof: Published proof-coverage index across the trust layer
headline: Procurement should anchor the trust-evidence stack.
nextMove: Publish the proof-coverage index and lock this assertion as the reference lane.

boardQuestion: Which revenue-system claims are still too early for external trust language?
publicAssertion: Reporting, attribution, and release controls already form one diligence-grade revenue control plane.
evidenceReality: Cross-surface evidence is thinner than the strength of the integrated-control narrative.
driftHeadline: Revenue should be framed as an emerging capability, not a closed trust assertion.
driftSignal: Cross-surface evidence is thinner than the strength of the integrated-control narrative.
missingProof: One end-to-end evidence packet connecting reporting, attribution, and release controls
headline: Revenue controls need a stronger proof chain before external trust reuse.
nextMove: Restate the integrated assertion until one linked revenue-control packet exists.

boardQuestion: Which regulated assertions need provenance reinforcement before reuse?
publicAssertion: GxP, specimen, CAPA, safety, and narrative review already form a reusable regulated trust packet.
evidenceReality: Evidence dates and repeatability notes are weaker than the confidence of the regulated trust narrative.
driftHeadline: The regulated trust packet is real, but still needs clearer provenance to feel durable.
driftSignal: Evidence dates and repeatability notes are weaker than the current trust narrative.
missingProof: Fresh provenance notes and one repeatable regulated packet
headline: Regulated controls need a provenance refresh before they read like a durable trust packet.
nextMove: Refresh provenance notes and publish one repeatable regulated packet.
